findings.evidence: Line 51 has `@[expose] def directingMeasure`; there is no `directingMeasure_apply`/`directingMeasure_def` grep hit besides the definition, while nearby APIs expose normal forms like `blockLaw_apply`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` and add a public normal-form lemma such as `@[simp] theorem directingMeasure_apply ... : directingMeasure μ X ω = @condDistrib ...` for the intended rewrite.
findings.issue: `directingMeasure` is marked `@[expose]`, so consumers can unfold the body instead of using a stable API.

findings.evidence: Lines 64-70 conclude `Measurable fun ω => directingMeasure μ X ω B` by downgrading via `tailProcess_le_ambient`; Mathlib `measurable_condDistrib` is explicitly sub-σ-algebra measurable, and the roadmap names tail/exchangeable measurability as the directing-measure target.
findings.fix: Add the stronger lemma `Measurable[tailProcess X] fun ω => directingMeasure μ X ω B` (derivable directly from `measurable_condDistrib`), and make the current ambient lemma a corollary using `tailProcess_le_ambient`.
findings.issue: The only measurability lemma for set evaluations states ambient `Measurable`, losing the characteristic `tailProcess X`-measurability of the conditional law.

summary: The public surface currently relies on unfolding the new definition and drops the tail-measurability property that characterizes this conditional law. Add characteristic lemmas so downstream de Finetti code can use the object without exposing its body.
